title: Cepaea caesalpini
taxonomy: Cepaea caesalpini, also known as the brown-lipped banded snail, belongs to the family Helicidae and is classified as follows: kingdom Animalia, phylum Mollusca, class Gastropoda, order Stylommatophora, and family Helicidae. This classification places it within the group of terrestrial snails, which are characterized by their ability to withdraw into their shells. The brown-lipped banded snail is further divided into several subspecies, each with distinct characteristics. It is native to Europe and parts of Asia, where it inhabits a variety of environments, including woodlands and grasslands. The classification of Cepaea caesalpini reflects its unique characteristics and adaptations to its environment.
